close_reason: Acknowledged: async-bridging design is correct, defend against simplification
description: The sign_sync function correctly handles both calling contexts: outside a tokio runtime (builds a current-thread runtime on the current thread) and inside a tokio runtime (spawns a scoped thread with its own runtime to avoid a nested block_on panic). Using std::thread::scope ensures the spawned thread cannot outlive the stack frame, which eliminates any lifetime or Arc leakage concern. This is the right solution to the sync-over-async bridge problem and should be defended if someone suggests replacing it with tokio::task::block_in_place (which would fail outside a multi-thread runtime) or a lazy_static runtime (which would conflict with caller-owned runtimes).
title: Async-bridging strategy is genuinely well-designed
